description_long: This object is a semi-automatic pistol with a distinctive cracked, stone-like texture in shades of brown and tan, giving it a unique, weathered appearance. The frame and slide are robust, with angular lines and a prominent barrel, typical of a Desert Eagle-style handgun. The grip is textured and ergonomically shaped, with a visible trigger guard and a black trigger. The rear of the slide features a hammer and safety lever, while the front end is squared off with a visible muzzle opening. The sights are integrated into the top of the slide, with a front post and a rear notch. The overall construction suggests a combination of metal and polymer materials, with the exterior finish mimicking a stone or mineral surface.
description: A large, stone-textured semi-automatic pistol with angular lines, a prominent barrel, and integrated iron sights. The grip and frame are robust, with a weathered brown and tan finish.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full profile, including the grip, trigger, slide, and barrel with the stone-like texture.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the muzzle opening and the width of the slide, with the stone pattern visible on the barrel.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the grip, trigger, and slide details, mirroring the first side but from the left.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the hammer, rear sight notch, and the back of the grip, with the stone texture continuing throughout.
